question_title: Will the interest in “trump magic paint” change between 2026-04-14 and 2026-04-25 according to Google Trends?
summary:
The "trump magic paint" search term spiked to 100 on Apr 12 (when news broke about Trump wanting to paint the Eisenhower Building with "magic paint") and has already decayed to 2 by Apr 14 (the start date). With a start value of 2 and a ±3 threshold:

- **Decreases is essentially impossible** — would require a value below -1, but Google Trends minimum is 0.
- **"Doesn't change" covers values 0-5** on Apr 25 — a wide band that encompasses the entire likely baseline range.
- **"Increases" requires value ≥ 6** on Apr 25 specifically.

The CFA presentation on the Eisenhower Building painting is scheduled for April 16, which is a real catalyst that could cause a follow-up spike. However, the observed decay from the initial spike (100→11→2 in 2 days) suggests that even a secondary spike would decay well before Apr 25 (9 days later). The term was at 0 for 2+ months before the spike, and the baseline expectation is a return to 0-2. "Doesn't change" is strongly favored by both the arithmetic (wide qualifying band at low start value) and the decay dynamics.

factors.description: Start value is 2, making 'Decreases' mathematically impossible (needs value < -1, Google Trends min is 0)
Spike already decayed: 100 (Apr 12) → 11 (Apr 13) → 2 (Apr 14). Classic post-spike rapid decay back to baseline. Term was at 0 for 2+ months before spike.
3-month base rate: 95.6% of day-to-day transitions show no change (>±3). Strong prior favoring 'Doesn't change'.
factors.conditional: Base rate dominated by zero-value periods, may not apply post-spike

factors.description: CFA presentation on Eisenhower Building painting scheduled for April 16 - confirmed catalyst within forecast window that could renew 'trump magic paint' searches
factors.conditional: Only matters if CFA presentation generates significant media coverage using 'magic paint' framing

factors.description: Even if CFA presentation causes a spike on Apr 16-17, Apr 25 is 9 days later - the Apr 12 spike decayed from 100 to 2 in just 2 days. A secondary spike would likely decay similarly before Apr 25.
Resolution scale is anchored to Apr 12 peak (100). Even a modest follow-up spike needs to reach >5 (5%+ of peak) specifically on Apr 25 to count as 'Increases'.
SerpAPI vs pytrends measurement uncertainty: values can differ by 1-3 points. At low absolute values this matters, but even with +3 shift, a baseline 0-2 value stays within 'Doesn't change' range.

reasoning: The reflection passed with approval. The reviewer verified all data points and found the reasoning sound. Let me now produce my final output.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “trump magic paint” change between 2026-04-14 and 2026-04-25 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43198)
[Trump proposes covering executive office building's gray stone facade with white paint](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/trump-proposes-painting-executive-office-building-white/)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=trump%20magic%20paint&date=2026-03-26%202026-04-25](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=trump%20magic%20paint&date=2026-03-26%202026-04-25) for 2026-04-25 is within 3 of the value at 2026-04-14. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "trump magic paint",
  "date": "2026-03-26 2026-04-25",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
